summary.intent: Fix accessibility gaps in ars-ui PR #682 by wiring programmatic labels (aria-labelledby) to meter and progress range roots, addressing Codex P1 review findings.
summary.outcome: Successfully added aria-labelledby wiring from range roots to visible labels for both meter and progress components, updated integration tests, accepted new insta snapshots, passed all local checks (fmt, clippy, spec validation, proptests), and committed/pushed the changes.

repo: ars-ui uses cargo insta for snapshot testing; snapshot changes from API output changes must be explicitly accepted with `cargo insta accept`
The project uses `cargo xtask spec validate` and `cargo xtask lint snapshot-count` as custom validation steps beyond standard clippy/fmt
PR #682 targets fogodev/ars-ui and uses Codex (@codex) as an automated reviewer triggered via `gh pr comment 682 --body '@codex review'`
Review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutations; multiple threads can be batch-resolved in one mutation

The meter/progress core contract deliberately keeps `role="meter"` and ARIA value attrs in `root_attrs()` with host-specific filtering left to adapters — this is an intentional API boundary for issues #268/#274
Range role components (meter, progress) require a programmatic accessible name via aria-labelledby when a visible label is present — this is enforced as P1 by Codex review

code.finding: meter root_attrs() was updated to emit aria-labelledby pointing at the label element's id; label_attrs() was updated to emit an id field
progress root_attrs() was updated to emit aria-labelledby; label_attrs() emits id for the label element

Integration tests were extended with assertions that meter root points at disk-label id and progress root points at upload-label id; tests were written as regression expectations before patching production code (red-green flow)

workflow: The session follows a strict red-green flow: write failing tests first, then patch production code
CI polling is done manually via `sleep N && gh pr checks` loops; Formatting and Snapshot Change Check run first, then Clippy, Workspace Check, Mutual Exclusion Guard, and finally Unit Tests
When pushing back on out-of-scope review requests, a clear reference to the existing issue plan and contract scope is used as justification in the thread reply
Co-author trailers are used to attribute Codex contributions in commits

friction: CI queue delays caused multiple polling cycles (sleep 120, sleep 180, sleep 240) before Clippy and Workspace Check jobs started running
Snapshot tests required a separate `cargo insta accept` step after implementation changes, adding a manual acceptance step to the workflow
Multiple rounds of waiting on CI between each stage of the review cycle (format → clippy → unit tests) with no event-driven notification
open_items: Host-specific filtering/remapping of meter root_attrs (role, ARIA value attrs, native value/min/max) is explicitly deferred to adapter PRs #268/#274 — the core contract intentionally does not branch by rendered host
Codex review pass was re-triggered but results not yet seen in the transcript — further review findings may require follow-up fixes
CI Unit Tests matrix was still pending at the end of the session
